title: O-1 Visa Lawyer for O-1A & O-1B | Colombo & Hurd
content:
## O-1 Visa Lawyers: Your Swift & Flexible Pathway to the U.S.
Unlock a swift, flexible pathway to the U.S. with the O-1 visa —designed for individuals with extraordinary ability or achievement in science, arts, education, business, athletics, or entertainment. The O-1 offers flexibility, speed, and opportunity, especially for professionals in STEM.
- Expedited approval and no visa backlog
- Unlimited extensions
- No annual cap or limitation by country

## More than 20 years of Excellence in Immigration Law
The O-1 visa is a temporary, non-immigrant, visa for individuals with extraordinary ability in the sciences, arts, education, business, or athletics, or for individuals who have demonstrated extraordinary achievement in the motion picture or television industry. This visa, sponsored by a U.S. employer or agent, is designed for top-tier talent who have earned national or international recognition in their field.
With an initial stay of up to three years and the option for unlimited extensions, the O-1 offers flexibility and speed, especially with premium processing available for approvals in 15 days. The O-1 visa also allows dependents (spouses and unmarried children under 21) to accompany visa holders to the U.S. under the O-3 category. For accomplished professionals looking to bring their talents to the U.S., the O-1 is a swift and flexible pathway.
- Unlimited extensions
- Immediate family members can accompany you

## O-1A Visa Requirements
For the O-1A visa , the beneficiary must provide evidence of a widely recognized achievement (like an Academy Award, Pulitzer Prize, or Nobel Prize) or meet at least three of the following eight criteria:
- Nationally recognized prizes or awards
- Membership in exclusive organizations
- Judging or panelist experience
- Original contributions of major significance
- High salary or compensation

## O-1B Visa Requirements
For the O-1B visa , the beneficiary must provide evidence of a widely recognized achievement (like an Academy Award, Pulitzer Prize, or Nobel Prize) or, more commonly, meet at least three of the following six criteria:
- Lead or starring role (productions or events)
- Lead or critical role (organizations)
- National/international recognition
- Major commercial or critical success
- Recognition from exclusive organizations
- High salary or compensation

## Frequently Asked Questions about the O-1 Visa
The evidence required to demonstrate “extraordinary ability “ or “extraordinary achievement” varies depending on the individual’s field. The USCIS defines “extraordinary ability” differently for each industry. In science, education, business, and athletics, it means being among the top experts in the field. In the arts, it means achieving distinction and prominent recognition. For the motion picture and television industry, “extraordinary achievement” means receiving recognition as a notable or leading professional.
To apply for an O-1 visa , first review the requirements and ensure you meet the eligibility criteria. Consult an O-1 immigration attorney for guidance. The petitioner files Form I-129O, Petition for a Nonimmigrant Worker, O Classifications, along with required evidence, at least 45 days before the scheduled employment start date. Then, the petitioner and beneficiary wait for USCIS review. If approved, USCIS notifies the petitioner using Form I-797: Notice of Action. After approval, the beneficiary can apply for an O-1 visa at a U.S. embassy or consulate. Upon receiving the visa, they can enter the U.S. and begin work or performance activities.
O-1 visa holders can extend their stay in increments of up to one year, with no limit on the number of extensions. To extend their stay, the petitioner must submit a new Form I-129, a copy of the beneficiary’s Form I-94, and a statement explaining the reasons for the extension. Dependents, including spouses and unmarried children under 21, must file Form I-539 to extend or change their nonimmigrant status.
The O-1 visa category will depend on the profession of the O-1 individual.
The O-1A extraordinary ability visa is designated for individuals with an extraordinary ability in the sciences, education, business, or athletics.
The O-1B visa is designated for individuals with extraordinary ability in the arts or extraordinary achievement in the motion picture or television industry.
When selecting an O-1 lawyer, it’s important to choose a team with extensive experience , a proven track record , and a deep understanding of immigration law.
